title: Fryxellia pygmaea

This family is part of the order Myrtales, which includes several other families of flowering plants. Fryxellia pygmaea is a species within the genus Fryxellia, which is endemic to Mexico. The genus Fryxellia is further classified within the tribe Melastomeae, one of the main tribes within the family Melastomataceae. Further classification of Fryxellia pygmaea at the family, order, and genus levels is reflective of its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Fryxellia pygmaea, also known as Pygmaea or Small Fryxellia, is a small, shrubby plant belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Fryxellia pygmaea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Fryxellia pygmaea is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
